decisions.topic: Primary orchestration model
options_considered: Human-only orchestration
Antigravity lead orchestration
Peer-to-peer self-coordination
decisions.selected_option: Antigravity lead orchestration
decisions.rationale: Single lead minimizes ambiguity and merge-order confusion across concurrent agents.

decisions.topic: Default collaboration topology
options_considered: Feature branches per epic
Trunk with short branches
Patch queue without branches
decisions.selected_option: Trunk with short branches
decisions.rationale: Continuous small merges are the strongest conflict reducer for this pace.

decisions.topic: Ownership strictness
options_considered: No ownership
Soft ownership
Strict folder ownership
decisions.selected_option: Strict folder ownership
decisions.rationale: Explicit lane boundaries reduce overlap and integration risk.

decisions.topic: Worktree execution model
options_considered: Single checkout with branches only
Worktree optional
Mandatory worktree per task
decisions.selected_option: Mandatory worktree per task
decisions.rationale: Per-task filesystem isolation reduces branch and dirty-tree collisions across concurrent agents.

decisions.topic: Scope expansion for T-20260211-001 Expo bootstrap files
options_considered: Keep narrow scope and create non-runnable shell fragments
Expand scope to include minimal Expo root config files
decisions.selected_option: Expand scope to include minimal Expo root config files
decisions.rationale: A runnable Expo Router shell requires root config files outside app/(tabs).

decisions.topic: Cross-lane hotfix to analytics provider parse/type signature
options_considered: Leave provider parse issue and ship onboarding analytics without type checks
Apply minimal provider hotfix while implementing T-20260211-003
decisions.selected_option: Apply minimal provider hotfix while implementing T-20260211-003
decisions.rationale: Unblocked onboarding analytics imports by fixing a malformed doc comment and navigationRef addListener type in provider.tsx without changing analytics runtime behavior.

decisions.topic: Global typography enforcement policy
options_considered: Document-only typography guidance
Hard policy plus automated validator
decisions.selected_option: Hard policy plus automated validator
decisions.rationale: Prevents recurring font misuse by making Zalando default UI mandatory and enforcing restricted use of Lora/Playfair/Amiri via script.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Journey streak redesign
options_considered: Wait for a claude-lane implementer to take Journey changes
Allow codex to execute the Journey rework in an isolated task worktree
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute the Journey rework in an isolated task worktree
decisions.rationale: The user requested immediate implementation and the work can remain fully isolated to src/features/journey/** without expanding into shared navigation or settings files.

decisions.topic: Allow larger diff for T-20260307-022 Journey state and UI rewrite
options_considered: Split Journey rework into separate storage and UI tasks
Allow one larger isolated task because the old routine-first model had to be replaced coherently
decisions.selected_option: Allow one larger isolated task because the old routine-first model had to be replaced coherently
decisions.rationale: The user requested a full Journey/Streaks restructuring, and splitting the storage migration from the UI rewrite would have prolonged an incoherent intermediate state.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Tools and Journey UI title polish
options_considered: Leave the task to the default owning lanes and wait for reassignment
Allow codex to execute a narrow cross-surface polish pass after explicit human approval
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a narrow cross-surface polish pass after explicit human approval
decisions.rationale: The requested change is a small, tightly scoped UI pass across the tools tab screen and journey feature components, and the human explicitly authorized reassignment to keep momentum.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Home hadith card polish
options_considered: Wait for an opencode-lane implementer to adjust the Home quote surface
Allow codex to execute a one-file Home hadith polish pass
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a one-file Home hadith polish pass
decisions.rationale: The requested change is tightly scoped to a single home feature component and can be completed safely without widening the diff or blocking on lane reassignment.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Tools and Journey card rebalance
options_considered: Split the redesign by ownership lane and wait for separate implementers
Allow codex to execute one cohesive redesign pass across the Tools hero and Journey hub surfaces
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute one cohesive redesign pass across the Tools hero and Journey hub surfaces
decisions.rationale: The requested redesign is intentionally about shared visual language between the Tools and Journey cards, so splitting it across lanes would increase iteration risk and produce a less coherent result.

decisions.topic: Product direction reset to imaan.app native intervention product
options_considered: Continue extending the Path of Nur PWA roadmap
Re-baseline the repo around the imaan.app PRD and native-first architecture
decisions.selected_option: Re-baseline the repo around the imaan.app PRD and native-first architecture
decisions.rationale: The product primitive, app IA, and backend needs have changed materially. Continuing to extend the old roadmap would create compounding architectural debt.

decisions.topic: Backend platform baseline for imaan.app
options_considered: Firebase-centered stack
Supabase with Azure-hosted app server
VM-only custom backend
decisions.selected_option: Supabase with Azure-hosted app server
decisions.rationale: Supabase is the system of record for auth, relational data, storage, and vectors, while Azure Container Apps hosts the orchestration API and workers using existing credits. OpenRouter is server-side only.

decisions.topic: Cross-boundary exception for repo re-baseline and backend foundation
options_considered: Split the repo reset across multiple owners before any implementation
Allow codex to execute one coordinated reset task across AGENTS, collab state, shared contracts, and backend scaffold
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute one coordinated reset task across AGENTS, collab state, shared contracts, and backend scaffold
decisions.rationale: The repo instructions, task queue, and code architecture all encode the old product. A single coordinated reset is the fastest way to stop new work from landing on stale assumptions.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for lean corpus seeding implementation
options_considered: Wait for opencode to take the server/shared corpus task
Allow codex to implement the corpus scripts and runtime retrieval schema immediately
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to implement the corpus scripts and runtime retrieval schema immediately
decisions.rationale: The user wants a lean local-script flow now, and the work stays tightly scoped to server/shared/docs. A single pass avoids building the wrong ingestion architecture.

decisions.topic: Cross-boundary exception for monorepo landing app setup
options_considered: Keep the repo single-app and add static marketing files at root
Allow codex to introduce a separate Vite landing app with minimal root monorepo plumbing
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to introduce a separate Vite landing app with minimal root monorepo plumbing
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly wants a separate Vercel root for the landing page. A small workspace-style setup isolates marketing deploys from the Expo app and reduces future git churn without forcing a full repo move.

decisions.topic: Hadith corpus safety policy for the lean retrieval seed
options_considered: Infer or fabricate per-hadith grades from an ungraded JSON dataset
Block the corpus task until a graded hadith dataset is sourced
Use a conservative collection whitelist with explicit documentation of the dataset limitation
decisions.selected_option: Use a conservative collection whitelist with explicit documentation of the dataset limitation
decisions.rationale: The chosen hadith-json dataset does not expose per-hadith grade metadata. The safe MVP path is to seed only explicitly whitelisted collections by default and keep grade inference out of the pipeline.

decisions.topic: Cross-boundary exception for full monorepo cleanup
options_considered: Leave the mixed root layout in place while continuing feature work
Allow codex to execute a single structural cleanup moving mobile, api, landing, and contracts into apps/packages
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a single structural cleanup moving mobile, api, landing, and contracts into apps/packages
decisions.rationale: The user wants the repo cleaned before it grows further. A one-pass structural move is safer now than allowing more feature branches to land on a mixed root layout that will immediately be invalidated.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for landing waitlist page implementation
options_considered: Wait for the default apps/landing owner to take the page work
Allow codex to implement the waitlist one-pager directly in apps/landing after explicit human direction
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to implement the waitlist one-pager directly in apps/landing after explicit human direction
decisions.rationale: The human explicitly asked to start the landing page now. Keeping the task scoped to apps/landing only preserves isolation while avoiding unnecessary handoff delay.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for api seed wiring and env alias support
options_considered: Wait for the default api lane owner to pick up env and seeding work
Allow codex to execute a narrow apps/api-only slice so the first live seed can happen immediately
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a narrow apps/api-only slice so the first live seed can happen immediately
decisions.rationale: The user wants the first real seed path wired now. The work is tightly scoped to the api package plus collab state and is a direct continuation of the corpus pipeline already implemented by codex.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for stable retrieval API slice
options_considered: Wait for the default api lane owner to implement request-time retrieval
Allow codex to extend the freshly landed corpus work with one narrow retrieval route slice
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to extend the freshly landed corpus work with one narrow retrieval route slice
decisions.rationale: The user wants the next backend loop now, and the work is a direct continuation of the seeded corpus path. Keeping it scoped to apps/api, contracts, and collab state minimizes coordination risk.

decisions.topic: Cross-boundary exception for contracts package normalization and intervention endpoint
options_considered: Leave the temporary contracts bridge in place and build the intervention route on top of it
Allow codex to normalize shared package resolution and continue directly into the first intervention endpoint
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to normalize shared package resolution and continue directly into the first intervention endpoint
decisions.rationale: The worktree-specific import bridge is technical debt in a shared contracts package. Fixing resolution requires touching the worktree setup path as well as the package itself, and then landing the first non-501 intervention route is the shortest path to a stable backend surface.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for intervention persistence and ledger read path
options_considered: Wait for the default api lane owner to add Supabase persistence after the intervention route merge
Allow codex to continue directly into the persistence slice so the backend loop becomes durable immediately
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to continue directly into the persistence slice so the backend loop becomes durable immediately
decisions.rationale: The intervention route is now merged and functioning, but it is still transient. Persisting interventions and exposing a real ledger path is the next production-critical backend slice and remains tightly scoped to api, contracts, and collab state.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for authenticated actor linkage on intervention records
options_considered: Keep session-only ownership until a later auth phase and avoid touching mobile auth/session code
Allow codex to align backend persistence with the existing auth/session seams now so durable records stop depending on transient session ids
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to align backend persistence with the existing auth/session seams now so durable records stop depending on transient session ids
decisions.rationale: Session-only ownership is not a production endpoint model for durable memory. This slice needs narrow cross-boundary access to api, contracts, and the existing mobile auth/session seams so identity becomes consistent before more backend behavior lands.

decisions.topic: Remove legacy session ownership and prevent control-plane drift
options_considered: Keep actor_session_id as a deprecated nullable legacy column
Remove session ownership from the schema entirely and tighten repo rules so fallback/deprecation scaffolding and stale control-plane validators are not allowed to drift
decisions.selected_option: Remove session ownership from the schema entirely and tighten repo rules so fallback/deprecation scaffolding and stale control-plane validators are not allowed to drift
decisions.rationale: The app has no live users depending on the earlier session-only schema. Keeping the legacy ownership column and allowing stale validation logic to persist would create avoidable technical debt and contradictory repo behavior.

decisions.topic: Cross-lane exception for Home intervention entry rebuild
options_considered: Wait for the default home lane owner to replace the legacy dashboard surface
Allow codex to execute a narrow Home-only rebuild so the intervention-first mobile flow can start immediately
decisions.selected_option: Allow codex to execute a narrow Home-only rebuild so the intervention-first mobile flow can start immediately
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly wants the Home transaction rebuilt now. Keeping the task scoped to apps/mobile/src/features/home only avoids collision with the active auth/session and backend tasks while unblocking the core product surface.
